what_it_does: Acceptance test for Suite.start_guide() and per-step UI target resolution; verifies a system-initiated guided walkthrough binds the existing review-organ stepper over registered ui:// element addresses, presents corpus-authored how-to narration without calling a model, advances through next() to done, rejects unregistered addresses safely, and preserves the existing inbox review walkthrough behavior.
observations: Top-level script executes a guided-sequence acceptance suite with PASS/FAIL checks.
Defines check() helper that increments PASS or raises SystemExit(1).
Defines fresh_suite() to build a Suite with a discovered NodeRegistry and temp FsStore.
Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1 for inbox-hygiene parity with sibling suites.
Validates start_guide() returns organ_started, guide flag, session id, cursor 0, walkthrough mode, and address-based steps.
Verifies present_current() for guide steps returns corpus framing, raw.ui_target/raw.guide_address, how_to bundle, and top-level ui_target.
Walks the full sequence via next() and checks the done terminal.
Tests _registry_ui_target() honors registered guide_address/ui_target and falls back to ui://chrome/inbox for unregistered ones.
Tests fail-loud behavior when a guide topic has no registered addresses.
Includes regression checks that start_session over inbox items and start_walkthrough nothing-pending path remain unchanged.
standouts: Proves the guided mode is model-free by reusing the same organ and returning before coa().
G-43 per-step resolvable ui_target is validated against the live UI registry.
Fail-loud design: an empty topic returns organ_started:False with a reason rather than silently no-oping.
conventions: Follows the sibling-suite pattern: COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1, fresh_suite() factory, check() helper.
Uses global PASS counter and raises SystemExit on first failure.
Heavy inline comments explain each test section and tie to design concepts (C1, G-41, G-43, D1).
concerns: Tests call internal methods (start_guide, present_current, next, _guide_sequence, _registry_howto_for, _registry_ui_target) directly, coupling acceptance to implementation details.
Global PASS and SystemExit make it hard to run under a normal test runner without modification.
Mutates su3.GUIDE_SEQUENCES in place to inject an empty topic, relying on dict copy to avoid side effects.
Assumes specific seeded addresses (ui://toolbar/run, ui://inbox/build-review, ui://chrome/inbox) exist in the registry.
notes: This is the acceptance harness that demonstrates C1 guided tours are model-free by piggybacking the existing review organ over registered UI addresses.
questions: What is the exact shape of the payload returned by present_current() and next() for guide steps?
How are GUIDE_SEQUENCES populated and where is the default tour defined?
What does _registry_howto_for() return when an address has no authored howto?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it exercises the full lifecycle of a system-initiated guided sequence and G-43 resolution while preserving the review walkthrough path.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish that a new 'show me how' guided mode can be added without a parallel stepper or model dependency, by treating registered UI element addresses as review items and pulling corpus how-to text for narration.
novelty: It treats ui:// element addresses as first-class review-session items and validates that the same next()/present_current() lifecycle can serve both inbox reviews and guided tours.
contribution: A concrete acceptance contract for system-initiated guided sequences, including registry-valid ui_target behavior, fail-loud empty-topic handling, and regression coverage for the existing review walkthrough.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test that exercises Suite.start_guide(), present_current(), and next() over registered ui:// addresses to prove a model-free, system-initiated guided walkthrough and G-43 per-step UI target resolution.
intention_for_embedding: To verify the guided-operation mode reuses the existing review organ, resolves corpus how-to narration per registered element, advances to done without a model, and preserves the inbox review walkthrough behavior.

symbols.does: Increments PASS if cond is truthy, otherwise prints FAIL and exits.
symbols.description: A simple assertion helper used throughout the acceptance script; it raises SystemExit(1) on the first failing check so the suite stops immediately.

symbols.does: Constructs a new Suite with a discovered NodeRegistry and a temporary FsStore.
symbols.description: Factory used before each test section to isolate state; it discovers nodes from the NODES directory and creates a fresh filesystem-backed store in a temp directory.
